# Header

This analysis explores the relationship between fuel efficiency (`mpg`) and engine displacement (`disp`) using the `mtcars` dataset. We examine whether larger engine sizes are associated with lower fuel efficiency by computing their correlation and visualizing the relationship.

The correlation between `mpg` and `disp` suggests a negative relationship, meaning that cars with larger engines tend to have lower fuel efficiency.

## Sub-Header

Here is a plot:

::: {#f34b1957 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd

# Load dataset (equivalent to R's mtcars)
mtcars = sns.load_dataset("mpg").dropna()

plt.figure()

sns.scatterplot(data=mtcars, x="mpg", y="displacement")

plt.title("Fuel Efficiency vs Engine Displacement")
plt.xlabel("Miles per Gallon (mpg)")
plt.ylabel("Engine Displacement")

plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-1.png){width=593 height=449}
:::
:::


The scatter plot shows a clear negative relationship between engine displacement and fuel efficiency. As displacement increases, miles per gallon tend to decrease, indicating that larger engines are generally less fuel efficient.
